title: Perdure Guard Context
description: The operating-contract packet an external coding agent reads each loop (`perdure guard context --json`). It is the machine-readable answer to "what am I allowed to do, what is failing, and what proves I am done" for one guard session. An agent should re-read it before every edit: the allow-lists are the gate. `verification_condition` proves the work is correct; `done_condition` is the only signal that authorizes claiming the task complete — it also requires the run to be finalized into the ledger.

goal.description: The goal name resolved from the repo's Perdurefile, e.g. FixFailingTests.

run_id.description: The guard session id, e.g. run_….

phase.description: Session phase: open, verified, committed, or aborted.

allowed_files.description: The fs.write globs the agent may edit; edits outside these are rejected at the gate and block commit.

allowed_commands.description: The only commands Perdure will run, matched verbatim (no globbing).

forbidden.description: A map of named prohibitions to a human-readable consequence, e.g. out_of_scope_writes / unallowlisted_commands.

current_failure.description: A summary of the most recent failing verify, or null if the last verify passed or none has run.

next_required_action.description: The single next step Perdure expects, e.g. "edit files within allowed_files, then run `perdure guard verify`".

verification_condition.description: The check that proves the work is correct (every required command passed, no out-of-scope write). Necessary but not sufficient to claim done — the run must still be committed.

done_condition.description: The exact check the agent must use to decide it is finished; do not claim done until it holds. Stronger than verification_condition: it also requires phase=committed (the run finalized into the ledger).

verified.description: True only when every required command passed AND no out-of-scope file changed.
